original_hypothesis.title: GTC Test Token transferFrom Bypasses Allowance Check Allowing Unauthorized Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The GTC test token's transferFrom function has the allowance check commented out ('// Removed allowance check for easy testing'). Any address can transfer any other address's GTC tokens without approval. While explicitly marked as test code, if this mock were accidentally deployed in a staging or production environment alongside IdentityStaking, any user's staking tokens could be stolen.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The transferFrom function performs _transferTokens(src, dst, amount) without checking or decrementing allowances[src][msg.sender]. This is an intentional test simplification but represents a complete ERC-20 compliance failure.
deployment_assumptions: GTC.sol is only used in testing and never deployed to production
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: GTC.sol header: 'THIS CONTRACT IS ONLY USED AS PART OF TESTING / THIS IS NOT PRODUCTION CODE'. deploy.ts deploys 'Lock' not GTC. Production uses real GTC token.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Comment: '// Removed allowance check for easy testing'; _transferTokens called without allowance verification

preconditions: GTC.sol test mock is deployed instead of production GTC token
Users hold GTC tokens in accounts

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No allowance check or decrement; direct _transferTokens(src, dst, amount)
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Anyone can call transferFrom(victim, attacker, victimBalance) to steal all tokens

analysis_questions: Is there any risk of the test GTC being used in staging/testnet with real value?
Does the test suite adequately distinguish test tokens from production?
Could CI/CD accidentally deploy this mock?
validation_requirements: Verify production deployment uses real GTC, not this mock
Confirm test environments don't hold value
Review deployment scripts for GTC references

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the test GTC mock is accidentally used in production, any attacker can drain any user's GTC balance by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) without any prior approval.
prerequisites: Test GTC deployed to production or value-bearing environment

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete theft of all user GTC balances

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by checking allowance before transferFrom

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
